title: Unplayable ball: your escape hatch
microFact: Anywhere except a penalty area, YOU alone can declare your ball unplayable — for one stroke, three escape routes.
lesson:
Ball wedged against a tree root? Buried in a bush? You may declare it UNPLAYABLE — your call alone, no one's approval needed. Costs ONE penalty stroke, then choose:

1. STROKE AND DISTANCE: replay from the previous spot.
2. BACK-ON-A-LINE: drop on the line from the hole through the ball's spot, as far back as you like.
3. LATERAL: drop within two club-lengths of the ball, no nearer the hole.

In a BUNKER, options 2 and 3 must stay in the sand — or take a fourth option: drop OUTSIDE the bunker back on the line for TWO penalty strokes total.

The amateur mistake isn't using this rule too much — it's not using it enough. Hacking three times at a ball under a bush costs three strokes and your composure; one penalty drop costs one. Knowing when to take your medicine is the difference between a blow-up hole and a bogey, and it reads as course intelligence to everyone watching.

quiz.question: Who decides whether your ball is unplayable?
quiz.explanation: It's entirely your call, anywhere except inside a penalty area. One stroke, three options.
